definition: Whether AI/automation can substitute for demographic headwinds at macro scale is the largest uncertainty in the long-run macro outlook. The base rate of technology-driven productivity accelerations lasting >10 years is low (arguably 2 episodes). If pronatalism fails and immigration is constrained, all demographic risk concentrates into AI as the sole remaining variable.
evidence: Base rate of technology-driven productivity accelerations >10 years: arguably 2 episodes (electrification 1920-1940, IT 1995-2005)
5-15 year transition period where labor scarcity AND automation anxiety coexist
If AI complements rather than substitutes labor, it amplifies scarcity
All demographic risk funnels into AI if pronatalism fails and immigration is constrained
open_questions: Is AI substitutive or complementary to human labor at macro scale?
What is the realistic timeline for AI to offset demographic labor decline?
Does concentration of demographic risk into a single variable (AI) create systemic fragility?
